doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_custom_get_significand (mpfr/src/stack_interface.c L39-L44). One-line C body: `return (void*) MPFR_MANT(x)` -- returns a pointer to the limb array storing the mantissa.

In the TS port the mantissa is a single MSB-aligned bigint (src/core.ts L113-L135), so 'get significand' means: return x.mant.

For singular values (NaN/Inf/Zero) the C function returns whatever pointer happens to be in the _mpfr_d slot -- the contents are uninitialised/meaningless. The TS schema has x.mant=0n for singulars (convention), but reading the mantissa of a singular value is logically undefined either way. The golden_driver restricts inputs to NORMAL values to avoid the meaningless-on-both-sides case.

Class 'misc'. Tag minimums per Rule 7 apply -- input domain is normal MPFR values across precisions.
divergence_from_c:
Two divergences:

  1. Return type: C `void *` (pointer to mp_limb_t array) -> TS `bigint` (MSB-aligned mantissa value). The C surface is a raw pointer, opaque to callers without companion prec/exp; the TS surface returns the actual mantissa bits as a single bigint, matching the schema convention.

  2. Singular inputs: C returns an opaque (and meaningless) pointer; TS would return 0n. Avoid the meaningless case by restricting golden_driver to NORMAL inputs; the TS port throws EDOMAIN on singular inputs.
